items.question_text: Complete: Under 7-6-1a, basic radar services for VFR aircraft must include safety alerts, traffic advisories, and limited radar _____ when requested by the pilot.
items.explanation: Per 7-6-1a, the three components of basic radar services are: (1) safety alerts, (2) traffic advisories, and (3) limited radar vectoring when requested by the pilot. Note that limited radar vectoring is provided only on pilot request.

choices.text: vectoring

items.question_text: Under 7-6-2a, what must the controller do on initial contact when basic radar service cannot be provided because of a radar outage?
items.explanation: Per 7-6-2a, when radar service cannot be provided due to radar outage, inform the aircraft on initial contact and apply the procedures in paragraph 7-1-3 (Approach Control Service for VFR Arriving Aircraft) as applicable.

choices.text: Inform the aircraft on initial contact and apply 7-1-3 procedures.
Terminate all services and instruct the aircraft to contact FSS.
Continue providing services using nonradar separation only.
Hand off the aircraft to the nearest facility with operational radar.

items.question_text: Under 7-6-4, what must be done before taking action to position an aircraft in the approach sequence?
items.explanation: Per 7-6-4, the aircraft must be identified before the controller takes any action to position it in the approach sequence. This is a fundamental safety requirement — you cannot sequence what you cannot identify.

choices.text: Identify the aircraft.
Coordinate with the tower for landing sequence.
Confirm the aircraft is IFR-equipped.
Obtain a pilot report of the airport in sight.

items.question_text: Under 7-6-5, where should VFR aircraft be held when holding is required to establish an approach sequence?
items.explanation: Per 7-6-5, VFR aircraft should be held over the initial reporting fix or a fix near the airport when holding is required to establish an approach sequence. This is different from the visual holding of 7-1-4 which uses geographical fixes.

choices.text: Over the initial reporting fix or a fix near the airport.
Only at depicted VFR checkpoints on sectional charts.
At the final approach fix for the runway in use.
In any quadrant chosen by the pilot for convenience.

items.question_text: Complete: Under 7-6-6, do not assign _____ sequence numbers when establishing aircraft in the approach sequence unless this responsibility has been delegated in a LOA or facility directive.
items.explanation: Per 7-6-6, landing sequence numbers are NOT to be assigned by approach control unless specifically delegated via LOA or facility directive. This is normally a tower function; approach control sequences but does not number.

choices.text: landing

items.question_text: Under 7-6-8a, what is the procedure for transferring control of a VFR aircraft to the tower?
items.explanation: Per 7-6-8a, the controller informs the tower of the aircraft's position and THEN instructs the pilot to contact the tower. This two-step process ensures the tower knows about the aircraft before the pilot calls.

choices.text: Instruct the pilot to contact the tower, then inform the tower.
Inform the tower of the aircraft's position, then instruct the pilot to contact the tower.
Transfer radar identification and instruct the pilot to squawk VFR.
Hand off the aircraft via the radar data block and await tower acceptance.

items.question_text: Under 7-6-9, what should the controller do when an aircraft under tower control abandons the approach?
items.explanation: Per 7-6-9, when an aircraft under tower control abandons the approach and coordination with approach control has not been established, instruct the aircraft to change to approach control for sequencing. This gets the aircraft back into the approach control sequence for another attempt.

choices.text: Instruct the aircraft to change to approach control for sequencing.
Cancel the aircraft's IFR flight plan because the approach was abandoned.
Instruct the aircraft to enter the traffic pattern for a visual approach.
Tell the aircraft to remain on tower frequency while the tower coordinates.

items.question_text: Complete: Under 7-6-12a, when the tower is not in operation, provide: wind direction and _____, and favored runway.
items.explanation: Per 7-6-12a1, when the tower is inoperative, the first thing provided is wind direction and velocity. Other items include: favored runway, altimeter setting, traffic advisories, and pertinent airport conditions.

choices.text: velocity

items.question_text: Under 7-6-3, when may a local controller position a sighted aircraft in the landing sequence at the time of first radio contact?

choices.text: Only after coordination with approach control has been effected.
Immediately, because the controller has visual contact with the aircraft.
Only for IFR aircraft that have been radar identified.
After the pilot reports the airport in sight and requests sequencing.

items.explanation: Per 7-6-3, a local controller may position a sighted aircraft in the landing sequence at first contact, but only after coordination with approach control has been effected.

items.question_text: Under 7-6-7a, what must be established before instructing a VFR aircraft to enter the traffic pattern at a specified point?

choices.text: Radar contact.
Two-way radio communication.
A pilot report of the airport in sight.
Coordination with the tower for the approach sequence.

items.explanation: Per 7-6-7a, radar contact must be established before the controller instructs a VFR aircraft to enter the traffic pattern at a specified point or vectors the aircraft to a position in the approach sequence.

items.situation: A VFR aircraft receiving basic radar service is approaching an airport other than the primary airport for landing. The aircraft is not requesting further services and the controller's workload is increasing.
items.para_context: Per 7-6-11, basic radar services should be provided to the extent possible, workload permitting. Terminate radar service to aircraft landing at airports other than the primary airport when the pilot has been advised.
items.question_text: When can the controller terminate basic radar service to this aircraft?

choices.text: When the aircraft is landing at an airport other than the primary airport and the pilot has been advised.
Only when the aircraft has radar contact with the destination airport's approach control.
Upon pilot request only; the controller cannot initiate termination of service.
At any time, because basic radar services are entirely workload-dependent and may be terminated without notice.

items.explanation: Per 7-6-11, service may be terminated to aircraft landing at airports other than the primary airport, provided the pilot has been advised. Service should otherwise be provided to the extent possible, workload permitting.

items.instruction: The controller provided VFR departure information. Which pilot readback is correct?
items.clearance: CONTACT DEPARTURE ON ONE TWO FOUR POINT FIVE FIVE AFTER PASSING TWO THOUSAND FEET.

choices.text: Contact departure one two four point five, N123AB.
After two thousand, we'll contact departure on one two four point five five.
Wilco, switching to tower now.
Roger, maintain two thousand until further advised.

items.explanation: Per 7-6-10, departing VFR aircraft requesting radar traffic advisories are told when to contact departure control and the frequency to use.
